help.title: Combat System
help.body:
Combat in Godless is real-time and automatic. Once engaged (via 'kill' or being attacked), you will auto-attack every few seconds. You can weave Skills and Spells into combat using commands like 'cast fireball' or 'kick'.

Combat is 'Sticky': You cannot switch targets freely without fleeing or defeating your current opponent. If you are attacked by multiple enemies, you will automatically switch to the next one if your target dies.

help.title: Resources
help.body:
HP: Your life force. If it hits 0, you die.
Chi: A specialized resource for martial abilities.
Favor: Divine currency earned by killing mobs. Used to buy Blessings from Deities.

help.title: The Blessing Deck
help.body:
Your abilities are determined by your active Class Kit. When you adopt a class (e.g. Monk, Knight), you are granted a specific set of Blessings and Gear known as a 'Kit'.

You can only use the blessings that belong to your current Kit. To change your available abilities, you must visit a Deity and swap your class.

help.title: Class Identity
help.body:
Your Class is defined by your active Kit. You can change your class by visiting a Deity and asking to 'become' a new archetype (e.g. 'become monk').

Each class comes with a pre-defined loadout of Gear and Blessings. You cannot mix-and-match abilities from different classes; you must fully embody the archetype.

help.title: Synergies
help.body: Synergies are passive bonuses inherent to certain Class Kits. They provide stat boosts or unique mechanics that reinforce the class's playstyle.

help.title: Security Levels
help.body:
The world is divided into security sectors based on distance from Capitals:

High-Sec (1.0 - 0.5): Near capitals. No PvP. Guards kill aggressors.
Low-Sec (0.4 - 0.1): Wilderness. PvP Allowed. Guards only at outposts.
Null-Sec (0.0): Deep wilds/Warzones. Lawless. Free-for-all PvP. Best loot.

help.title: Death
help.body: If you die, you leave behind a corpse containing your items. You will respawn at your Kingdom's capital (or the starting area). You must return to your corpse to retrieve your gear. In High-Sec, you keep your items. In Null-Sec, anyone can loot your corpse.

help.title: Crafting
help.body: You can craft items using materials found in the world. Use 'recipes' to see what you can make, and 'craft <item>' to create it. You need the ingredients in your inventory.

help.title: Travel & Mapping
help.body: Move using cardinal directions (n, s, e, w, u, d). Use 'map' to see a grid of the surrounding area. 'scan' reveals nearby enemies. 'recall' returns you to your capital city.

help.title: Commune
help.body: Use 'commune' at a shrine or near a Deity Avatar to enter a trance. While communing, you can 'become' new classes if you have enough Favor. This will automatically equip the class's Kit (Gear and Blessings).

help.title: Sylva, The Wild Mother
help.body: Sylva represents the untamed growth of nature and the wisdom of instinct. She is worshipped by druids, rangers, and those who reject the rigid order of Light or the nihilism of Dark. Her blessings focus on Wisdom, Nature, and Survival.

help.title: Krog, The Primal Force
help.body: Krog is the embodiment of raw physical power and the survival of the fittest. He cares not for morality, only strength. His followers are often barbarians and warriors who seek to crush their enemies with overwhelming force.

help.title: Nox, The Silent Blade
help.body: Nox is the patron of assassins, thieves, and those who operate in the shadows. He values precision and the element of surprise. His blessings enhance Dexterity, Stealth, and Critical Strikes.

help.title: Solara, The Sun Queen
help.body: Solara brings the blinding truth of the sun. She represents order, hierarchy, and the burning purity of Light. Her followers are zealots and mages who wield fire and holy energy to purge the world of darkness.

help.title: Umbra, The Void
help.body: Umbra is the emptiness between stars. She represents the arcane mysteries of the void and the power of the mind to unravel reality. Her blessings focus on Intelligence, Curses, and Anti-Magic.

help.title: Aurelius, The Golden Shield
help.body: Aurelius is the defender of the weak and the champion of justice. He represents the protective aspect of Light. His blessings focus on Constitution, Defense, and Healing.

help.title: The Three Kingdoms
help.body:
The world is divided into three primal philosophies:

LIGHT (Solas): Order, Civilization, Hierarchy. Capital: Sanctum.
DARK (Umbra): Ambition, Mystery, Individualism. Capital: Noxus.
INSTINCT (Sylva): Nature, Survival, Freedom. Capital: Ironbark.

help.title: Tag: Strike
help.body:
Type: Physical Scaling

Attacks with this tag are considered physical. Their damage effectiveness relies on your physical conditioning (Stamina/Stability).

help.title: Tag: Martial
help.body:
Type: Physical / Identity

The fundamental tag of physical combat. Used by Warriors, Monks, and Soldiers.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Physical (Stamina/Stability)
- Breakthrough: Reaching 10+ Martial voltage unlocks passive bonuses.
- Synergy: Triggers 'Crimson Charges' for Red Mages.

help.title: Tag: Lethal
help.body:
Type: Mechanic

Attacks designed to finish off vulnerable opponents.

[Mechanics]
- Critical: If the target is [Off Balance], this attack has double (2x) the chance to Crit.

help.title: Tag: Heavy
help.body:
Type: Physical / Weight

Represents massive, cumbersome power.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Physical (Stamina/Stability)
- Movement: If you have the 'Heavy' tag (e.g. from Armor), movement stamina cost is tripled (3x).

help.title: Tag: Weight
help.body:
Type: Mechanic

Attacks that use mass to overwhelm the target.

[Mechanics]
- Knockdown: If the target is [Off Balance], this attack will knock them [Prone].

help.title: Tag: Concussive
help.body:
Type: Mechanic

Attacks that rattle the target's senses or overheat their systems.

[Mechanics]
- Heat: Generates +15 Heat on the target.

help.title: Tag: Disruption
help.body:
Type: Mechanic

Attacks designed to break focus and interrupt casting.

[Mechanics]
- Drain: Removes 15 Concentration from the target.
- Bonus: Deals increased damage to targets suffering from [Shattered Mind].

help.title: Tag: Magic
help.body:
Type: Magical Scaling

Raw magical energy.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Magical (Concentration)
- Blocked By: [Mental Strain] and [Shattered Mind] status effects.

help.title: Tag: Arcane
help.body:
Type: Magical / Identity

The tag of pure, unstable magic used by Mages and Sorcerers.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Magical (Concentration)
- Breakthrough: Reaching 10+ Arcane voltage unlocks passive bonuses.

help.title: Tag: Holy
help.body:
Type: Magical / Identity

The tag of divine light used by Clerics and Paladins.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Magical (Concentration)
- Breakthrough: Reaching 10+ Holy voltage unlocks passive bonuses.

help.title: Tag: Dark
help.body:
Type: Magical / Identity

The tag of shadow and void used by Warlocks and Assassins.

[Mechanics]
- Scaling: Magical (Concentration)
- Breakthrough: Reaching 10+ Dark voltage unlocks passive bonuses.

help.title: Tag: Instinct
help.body:
Type: Primal / Identity

The tag of nature and rage used by Druids and Barbarians.

[Mechanics]
- Breakthrough: Reaching 10+ Instinct voltage unlocks passive bonuses.

help.title: Tag: Restoration
help.body:
Type: Healing

Magic dedicated to mending wounds.

[Mechanics]
- Penalty: If your Concentration is 0 or less (Mental Debt), healing effectiveness is reduced by 50%.

help.title: Tag: Concealed
help.body:
Type: Status / Mechanic

Represents being hidden from sight.

[Mechanics]
- Backstab: Attacking while Concealed deals 3.0x Damage.
- Removal: Moving (without Sneaking) or Attacking removes this tag.

help.title: Tag: Blunt
help.body:
Type: Physical

Crushing damage type.

[Mechanics]
- Bonus: Deals increased damage to targets suffering from [Shattered Mind].

help.title: Tag: Alchemy
help.body:
Type: Utility / Identity

The science of transmutation and brewing.

[Mechanics]
- Requirement: Used to unlock Alchemist class abilities.
- Scaling: Affects potency of crafted items and thrown flasks.

help.title: Status: Stunned & Dazed
help.body:
Stunned: You cannot take any actions. Movement, attacks, and casting are all disabled.
Dazed: You are off-balance and take increased damage from critical strikes.

help.title: Skill: Intervene & Rescue
help.body: Knight/Paladin abilities used to protect allies. Intervening redirects the target's attackers to yourself, forcing the enemies to engage you instead.

help.title: Beastmaster Pets
help.body: Beastmasters can recruit animals to fight alongside them. Use 'recruit' on a suitable beast. Pets share your room, follow you when you move, and automatically assist you in combat.

help.title: Skill: Sanctify
help.body: A Holy ability that purifies a target, removing negative status effects and dealing damage to undead or dark creatures.

help.title: Skill: Brace
help.body: A defensive maneuver that increases your resistance to all incoming physical damage for a short duration. Requires a certain amount of Martial voltage.

help.title: Skill: Magic Shield
help.body: An arcane barrier that absorbs a portion of incoming damage by consuming Mana instead of HP. Essential for glass-cannon casters.

help.title: Armor & Mitigation
help.body: Defense reduces incoming raw damage by a flat amount (Damage - Defense = Final Damage). Mitigation is a percentage-based reduction applied after defense. Heavier armor provides more defense but drains stamina more quickly when moving.

help.title: Class: Monk
help.body: The Monk is a high-agility martial artist who thrives on 'Flow'. By switching between stances like Crane (Defense/Wisdom) and Turtle (Immunity/Stability), the Monk maintains control of the battlefield. Their attacks often hit multiple times or apply rapid pressure via skills like Triple Kick and Iron Palm.

help.title: Class: Barbarian
help.body: The Barbarian is a relentless engine of destruction. Unlike other classes that rely on Mana or Chi, the Barbarian generates 'Momentum' through aggressive combat. Their abilities consume Momentum to unleash devastating AOE strikes like Whirlwind. High Momentum also provides passive extra attacks (3 pips = 1 extra hit, 5 pips = 2 extra hits).

help.title: Mechanic: Momentum
help.body: Momentum is the core resource of the Barbarian. It is generated automatically during combat as you land hits (Max 5). It decays after 6 seconds of inactivity. High Momentum empowers Whirlwind and grants passive multi-attacks.
